verdict_reasoning: SRC is canonically an inner-leaflet-anchored, N-myristoylated non-receptor tyrosine kinase. However, Delaveris et al. (Science 2026) demonstrated that in cancer cells overexpressing SRC, the protein is topologically inverted onto the outer plasma membrane via autophagolysosomal exocytosis (eSRC). eSRC was detected on bladder, colorectal, breast, and pancreatic tumor cells and primary patient tumors but not healthy tissue, and was successfully targeted by anti-Src antibodies, ADCs, BiTEs, and radioligand therapeutics in preclinical models. This qualifies as oncogenic-state-induced ecto-display.
